manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: water, lawyer, coffee, life, apartment, lot, birthday, rock, box.
Your opponent's team words are: moment, horse, minute, place, clothes, bar, house, baby.
Distractor words are: kids, hair, building, case, captain, class, miles.
The kill words are: day.

message.content:
CLUE: lease
TARGETS: apartment, lot, lawyer
message.reasoning:
The user wants a single-word clue and list of target words. Must be semantically related to some of my team's words (water, lawyer, coffee, life, apartment, lot... etc). Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Must be one word. Provide CLUE: ... TARGETS: ...

We need to avoid kill word day: clue can't be day. Also avoid opponent words.

Pick clue "brew" relates to coffee and maybe life? Maybe "brew" only coffee. Could relate also "lot" (brew lot?). Not good.

Maybe "lease" relates to apartment, lot, maybe lawyer (leases). That's good: lease (relates to apartment, lot, lawyer). Could also relate to water? Not really. ok.

Clue must be single word not containing parts: "lease" is okay (none of the words). Provide TARGETS: apartment, lot, lawyer. That's three.

Make sure clue not same as any word: lease not in list. Good.

Return format.
